{{Quote|I am detecting that the ARC fighters have engaged weapons tracking systems.|[[Darth Vader's droid]] to Darth Vader|<ref name="Darth Vader (2017) 2">[[Darth Vader (2017) 2|''Darth Vader'' (2017) 2]]</ref>}}
The '''Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 starfighter''', also known simply as the '''ARC-170 starfighter''', was a heavy-duty model of [[starfighter]] used by the [[Galactic Republic]] during the [[Clone Wars]] and was considered the latest in fighter technology. Jointly manufactured by [[Incom Corporation]] and [[Sublights Products Corporation|Subpro]], it continued to see usage into the [[Imperial Era|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire]], but was eventually phased out by the newer [[TIE series|TIE fighter series]], though some found their way into the hands of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Vectored_Thrusters_XWM.png|thumb|left|250px|An ARC-170 starfighter performing a barrel roll in atmosphere.]]

===Power and propulsion===
The Aggressive ReConnaissance-170 [[starfighter]] was a multipurpose [[starship]] measuring 12.71 [[meter]]s in length with a wingspan of 20 meters, designed for both intense battle and longer [[realspace|deep-space]] missions. It was equipped with a [[hyperdrive]]<ref name="Ships of the Galaxy" /> and propelled itself in realspace utilizing two powerful [[IGP-440a ion turbine engine]]s<ref name="Collapse" /> mounted in each of its two wings<ref name="Ships of the Galaxy" /> powered by a system of rear-mounted [[power generator]]s. These engines were equipped with [[retrorocket|retro-thrusters]] which, combined with a forward [[repulsorlift]] added to the ship's already impressive maneuverability<ref name="Complete Vehicles" /> and gave it a top speed of 1,050 [[kilometer per hour|kph]].<ref name="Ships of the Galaxy" /> To further aid in accelerating inside [[planet]]ary [[atmosphere]]s, the ARC-170's [[deflector shield]]s dispersed the heat generated by air resistance and enabled it to reach hypersonic speeds.<ref name="Complete Vehicles" />

===Armament and sensor package===
The ARC-170's main armament were two uncommonly large and powerful forward medium [[laser cannon]]s located on the underside of its outer wings<ref name="Ships of the Galaxy" /> and strong enough to punch through a [[capital ship]]'s [[armor]]<ref name="Ultimate Star Wars" /> including reinforced [[durasteel]] plating. A targeting [[rangefinder]] was mounted on each cannon and their muzzle could internally tilt the the [[laser]] beam by as little as a few degrees off-axis to allow for extremely precise shots.<ref name="Complete Vehicles" /> The starfighter was also armed with two rear-mounted [[blaster cannon]]s which provided a rear firing arc as well as [[proton torpedo]] [[Proton torpedo launcher|launchers]] which increased the craft's usefulness in ship-to-ship combat, and added to its already extensive repertoire in its ability to serve as a [[bomber]]. During battle, the craft's twin [[S-foils]] opened to expose [[heat sink]]s and active cooling [[radiator]] panels which helped cool the ship<ref name="Ultimate Star Wars" /> and improved its deflector shield's effectiveness.<ref name="Complete Vehicles" />

The ship's heavy nose section featured an impressive [[sensor]] package which included both active and [[passive sensor antenna|passive scanner modules]], a [[gamma ray]] imaging [[spectrometer|spectro-photometer]] and a [[dedicated energy receptor]].<ref name="Complete Vehicles" /> The latter was capable of scanning any [[electromagnet]]ic emissions within its range, from [[comlink]] transmissions to [[laser]] light,<ref name="YT1300">''[[YT-1300 Millennium Falcon Owners' Workshop Manual]]''</ref> allowing the ARC-170 to operate as a long-range [[scout]]. The ship was also equipped with a long-wave [[comlink|communication]] [[antenna]] and a [[Jammer|jamming device]].<ref name="Complete Vehicles" />

===Crew===
The ARC-170 was piloted by a crew of three—a [[pilot]], co-pilot, and tail [[gunner]], along with an [[astromech droid]]<ref name="Ultimate Star Wars" /> which doubled as a [[navigator]]. The co-pilot was also manning the forward weapons, although control could be turned over to the pilot. The ship was equipped with consumables for five [[standard day|days]], allowing the crew to undertake lonely independent missions deep into hostile territory and extending the Republic's reach beyond carrier ships and fleets.<ref name="Complete Vehicles" />

==History==
===Frontlines of the Clone Wars===
Developed shortly before the [[Clone Wars]],<ref name="BMF 36" /> the ARC-170 starfighter was initially created by [[Incom Corporation|Incom]]/[[Sublights Products Corporation|Subpro]] for the [[Galactic Republic]] as a multipurpose starship<ref name="Ships of the Galaxy" /> and saw deployment as soon as it was ready. It participated in numerous battles during the Clone Wars,<ref name="BMF 36" /> such as the [[mission to Quarmendy]], an early engagement of the war.<ref name="Battle Tales 2">[[Star Wars Adventures: The Clone Wars – Battle Tales 2|''Star Wars Adventures: The Clone Wars – Battle Tales'' 2]]</ref> However, it was a wartime modernization program that saw its wide-spread deployment across the [[Republic Navy|Galactic Republic Navy]],<ref name="Collapse" /> within which it rapidly replaced the [[V-19 Torrent starfighter]] as the navy's main [[Starfighter combat|dogfight]] vessel.<ref name="Encyclopedia of Starfighters and Other Vehicles" /> At the [[Battle of Malastare]],<ref name="ZB">{{TCW|The Zillo Beast}}</ref> ARC-170s guarded<ref name="BMF 36" /> a [[BTL-B Y-wing starfighter/bomber]] armed with the experimental [[electro-proton bomb]] while some strafed the Separatist ground forces.<ref name="ZB" /> They also participated in the [[Battle of Kamino]]<ref name="ARC Troopers">{{TCW|ARC Troopers (episode)}}</ref> and the [[Battle of Sullust]].<ref name="Nightsisters">{{TCW|Nightsisters (episode)}}</ref> ARC-170 Starfighters also took part in the [[Battle of Lola Sayu]], led by Jedi Masters [[Saesee Tiin]], [[Adi Gallia]], and [[Kit Fisto]], where they engaged [[Vulture-class starfighter|vulture droids]] and [[droid tri-fighter]]s in space, in order to break the blockade to allow [[Plo Koon]] and the [[Wolfpack]] to rescue Anakin, Obi-Wan, [[Ahsoka Tano]], and [[Wilhuff Tarkin]], who were under attack by [[Osi Sobeck]] and his droid escorts.<ref name="Rescue">{{TCW|Citadel Rescue}}</ref>

[[File:FrontLines-OddBall.png|thumb|right|300px|Clone Commander Davijaan flies an ARC-170 during the Battle of Coruscant]]
Later, several Wolfpack ARC-170 Starfighters were parked in [[Wolffe's Acclamator-class assault ship|Wolffe's ''Acclamator''-class assault ship]] prior to Wolfpack going on their mission to deliver supplies to the people of [[Aleen]], just after a series of groundquakes rocked the world, though the fighters never took part on the mission.<ref name="MM">{{TCW|Mercy Mission (episode)}}</ref> ARC-170 Starfighters and BTL-B Y-wing bombers also participated in a mission to rescue [[Adi Gallia]], who was being held captive by [[Grievous]]. The fighters assaulted the [[dreadnought]]s defenses so Plo Koon and his rescue team can board the ship to rescue Adi Gallia, along with [[C-3PO]] and [[R2-D2]], who were about to be incinerated to scrap metal by the Separatists.<ref name="ND">{{TCW|Nomad Droids}}</ref> Several ARC-170s also participated in the [[Battle of Umbara]] where they were involved in breaking through the Separatist blockade to clear way for General Kenobi, General Skywalker, and their clone forces to retake the planet.<ref name="Darkness">{{TCW|Darkness on Umbara}}</ref>

After seeing use during the campaign against the supposed "[[Admiral]] Enigma," who was actually the undercover Jedi Master [[Quinlan Vos]], and as part of the [[second battle of Christophsis]],<ref name="Dark Disciple">''[[Dark Disciple]]''</ref> ARC-170s played a major part in the [[Outer Rim Sieges]]. During the [[Battle of Coruscant]], [[Clone trooper|Clone]] [[Clone Commander|Commander]] "[[Davijaan]], or "Odd Ball," led [[Clone Flight Squad Seven]] to protect [[Anakin Skywalker]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi]] on their mission to rescue the [[Supreme Chancellor]]. Several participated in the [[Battle of Cato Neimoidia|capture]] of [[Cato Neimoidia]] and, following the battle, shot down [[Jedi General]] [[Plo Koon]] when [[Darth Sidious|Sheev Palpatine]] issued [[Order 66]].<ref name="Episode III" />

===Post-Clone Wars===
When the Clone Wars ended with the [[Proclamation of the New Order|Republic's transformation]] into the [[Galactic Empire]], ARC-170s naturally became part of the [[Imperial Navy]], much like how the clone troopers who flew them became [[stormtrooper]]s in the [[Imperial Military]]. As such, in the immediate aftermath of the war, several ARC-170s were used to keep the a former Jedi outpost known as the ''[[Brighthome]]'' under Imperial control as clones sorted through its inventory. When [[Darth]] Vader arrived at the station, he refused to give Imperial access codes to instead [[Attack on Brighthome|cut down]] the soldiers aboard, resulting in the ARC-170s moving to engage [[Vader's starship]] under the orders of one [[Unidentified clone pilot (Brighthome)|clone pilot]]. However, Vader quickly outmaneuvered the ARC-170s and destroyed them, leaving the clones aboard the station to assume he was a Jedi. Vader quickly boarded and cut through all the clones who came against him.<ref name="Darth Vader (2017) 2" />

[[File:Shara_Bey_ARC-170_XWM.png|thumb|left|250px|Shara Bey piloting an ARC-170 starfighter for the Rebellion]]
ARC-170s continued to see use in the Imperial Navy at least five years after the proclamation of the New Order as the newer [[TIE series|TIE fighter series]] came into production.<ref name="Tarkin" /> Squadrons of ARC-170s were deployed from an older-generation [[Venator-class Star Destroyer|''Venator''-class Star Destroyer]] to assist [[Governor]] [[Wilhuff Tarkin]] and the ''[[Executrix]]'' during the [[Salient Campaign|Campaign at Salient]].<ref name="Catalyst">''[[Catalyst: A Rogue One Novel]]''</ref> Several later ambushed [[Kanan Jarrus]] when he tried to return to the [[Imperial Palace|Jedi Temple]] on Coruscant despite Master Kenobi's warning not to. It also served as a predecessor of the [[T-65B X-wing starfighter]].<ref name="X-wing Fighter History Gallery">{{Databank|x-wing-fighter-history-gallery|X-wing Fighter History Gallery}}</ref>

The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]] also acquired a number of ARC-170s during the [[Galactic Civil War]], retrofitting them to Alliance standards. [[Shara Bey]], [[Thane Kyrell]], [[Braylen Stramm]], and [[Norra Wexley]] all flew the ARC-170 at some point in the war.<ref name="ARC-170 Expansion Pack" /> Shortly after the [[Battle of Hoth]], An ARC-170 was used by the [[Harlok Gang]].<ref name="Star Wars 2" /> [[Amidalans|Amidalan]] rebel cell also acquired several ARC-170s and used them against [[Darth]] Vader during the [[battle on Polis Massa]].<ref name="DV5" /> [[Jinata Security]] was known to use Clone Wars–era starfighters in its fleet as of [[34 ABY]]. The ARC-170, [[Alpha-3 Nimbus-class V-wing starfighter|V-Wing]], and [[N-1 starfighter]] were among its fleet of fighters.<ref name="Battlefront II" /> 

Between [[32 ABY]] and [[34 ABY]], one ARC-170 was piloted by [[Jessika Pava]] of the [[Resistance]]'s [[Black Squadron (Resistance)|Black Squadron]] during a [[Heist of the Alderaanian data archive|mission]] to obtain a [[Archive of the Great Library of Alderaan|data archive]] containing all that was left of the [[Royal Library of Alderaan|Royal Library]] of [[Alderaan]].<ref name="Poe Dameron Annual 2">[[Poe Dameron Annual 2|''Poe Dameron Annual'' 2]]</ref> 

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ARC170_concept.jpg|thumb|right|200px|ARC-170 concept art dated to the 1st August 2002.]]
The ARC-170 was named after "ART-170," the title of the artwork form by concept artist [[Ryan Church]] that inspired the finalized design. Originally, it was referred to simply as a "clone fighter" in the script for [[Star Wars: Episode III Revenge of the Sith|''Revenge of the Sith'']].

The [[Wikipedia: Head-up display|HUD]] seen on Odd Ball's ARC-170 cockpit during the Battle of Coruscant has the same [[Targeting computer|targeting display]] as [[Luke Skywalker]]'s X-wing in [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''A New Hope'']].<ref name="Episode III" /> An ARC-170 is seen on the cover of "[[Music Rainbow]]," which was designed for ''[[Star Wars Jedi: Survivor]]''<ref name="Art of Survivor">''[[The Art of Star Wars Jedi: Survivor]]''</ref> but cut from the final product.<ref name="Survivor">''[[Star Wars Jedi: Survivor]]''</ref> The design for the album's cover, however, would later be featured in ''[[The Art of Star Wars Jedi: Survivor]]''.<ref name="Art of Survivor" />
